vulnerabilities.description: Werkzeug interactive debugger is publicly accessible; page exposes a SECRET token and debug resources (?__debugger__ endpoints). With a valid PIN (often obtainable from server logs/env), this can lead to arbitrary code execution.
500 error returns full Werkzeug debugger traceback with source code and file paths (e.g., /app/app.py), leaking implementation details and internal paths.
Logout is implemented as a state-changing GET (@app.route('/logout', methods=['GET'])) with no CSRF protection, making it trivially triggerable via CSRF.

The login flow accepts a client-controlled 'role' parameter (hidden field) used to differentiate employee vs admin. If not strictly validated server-side, an attacker could set role=admin to influence authorization or redirects.
